notes:
dễ = easy, not difficult. It describes tasks, situations, people, or feelings.

As an adjective, it usually comes after a noun or after thì / là in simple statements.  
Very common pattern: dễ + verb to mean “easy to do” (Structure: dễ + V — dễ hiểu, dễ làm).

It can also mean “likely to” in some contexts, like something that can easily happen.  
Opposite: khó (hard) — a strong, useful pair to remember.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This exercise is quite easy so I finished it very quickly.
I think cooking this dish is not too easy but also not hard.
I like learning English because basic pronunciation is quite easy.

content.sentence: Bài học này rất ___ cho tôi.

translation.en: This lesson is very easy for me.
This lesson is very easy for me.

content.sentence: Bài học này rất ___ cho tôi.

exampleSentences: Bài tập này khá dễ nên em làm xong rất nhanh.
Anh thấy nấu món này không quá dễ nhưng cũng không khó.
Chị thích học tiếng Anh vì phát âm ở mức cơ bản khá dễ.
notes_md:
**dễ** = easy, not difficult. It describes tasks, situations, people, or feelings.

As an adjective, it usually comes after a noun or after **thì / là** in simple statements.  
Very common pattern: **dễ + verb** to mean “easy to do” (Structure: dễ + V — dễ hiểu, dễ làm).

It can also mean “likely to” in some contexts, like something that can easily happen.  
Opposite: **khó** (hard) — a strong, useful pair to remember.
